comments.body: agree, this is very annoying. and I'm too lazy to have tried to figure it out. if you have tried and failed to find a solution, time to file a bug to IntelliJ. I find that they are very responsive to user feedbacks.
There is a menu option Window/Store Current Layout as Default. I have not tried it but maybe it could do what you want?
That sounds close, but I still have to manually save and restore the "default". Which means I have to press save every time I close a project and press load every time I open/create a project.
@TWiStErRob I'm not aware of any better way. Try creating feature request on youtrack.jetbrains.com to allow automatically sharing window layout between all projects.

It's still manual, but I tried this and it kinda' works: some views are not moved to the correct places (e.g. Terminal was Bottom-Left when saved, but it restored to Bottom-Right; Run was Bottom-Left when saved, restored Top-Left). It also doesn't close extra open Views.
I'll report back if I open a request in YT.

answers.body:
The closest functionality that I know of is Window/Store Current Layout as Default. It will save the current layout and it will be used for newly created projects.

But when you modify the layout and want to use that version as default, you have to first save it as default using the action mentioned above and then in other projects use Window/Restore Default Layout (ie. the changes to default layout won't be saved/applied to other projects automatically).

I would suggest opening a feature request on https://youtrack.jetbrains.com to enable sharing the layout automatically between all projects.

comments.body: On my current version I save a layout. On the other version when I tried to located the created layout under Window -> Layout, I can only see custom and not the layout I saved with a different name.

answers.body:
Not so much an answer as a comment — for anyone coming across this question — that layouts are now shared among projects in IntelliJ-based IDEs, thus providing the solution that was lacking in 2015. This is true at least as of the 2025.1 versions which are current as I write this.

(I have observed a potential bug where using a saved layout does not "take" the first time around when opening a project. A workaround is to switch to Default layout, and then back to the custom one.)

Layouts are saved in the IDE's system-dependent configuration directory, in the file options/window.layouts.xml.

Clone window.layouts.xml Between IDE Versions

I still haven’t cracked a fully automated flow, but here’s my workaround: just clone the window.layouts.xml from one IDE install to the next.

  1. In your source IDE, goto Window -> Layout -> Save Current Layout as New... and give it a name.

  2. Locate the source layout file. On MacOS, it lives in the options folder of your IDE's settings.

/Users/<user_name>/Library/Application Support/JetBrains/WebStorm2025.1/options
  1. Copy the files contents into the target IDE. e.g:
cp ~/Library/Application\ Support/JetBrains/WebStorm2025.1/options/window.layouts.xml \
  ~/Library/Application\ Support/JetBrains/WebStorm2025.2/options/

  1. Open the destination IDE and goto Window -> Layout. You should see the layout name you saved in step 1.

  2. Select the layout and hit apply.

Now you should be able to view the layout(docked panels, sizes, positions, etc.) from your old IDE version to the new one.

title: How to share IDE layout in IntelliJ IDEA?
body:
I'm frequently changing projects and creating new ones, but I have a big a project that I work on a lot. The IDE layout (view positions, sizes and docking) evolves as I use it more and more. I'm looking for a way to automatically share IDE layout between projects in IDEA?

I would like to avoid re-setting up my environment every time I do File/New/Project. I also would like to avoid exporting/importing my configuration for every File/New/Project. Handling of updates would be a really nice one too, that is, when I resize the Project/Debug view in one project it changes in all of my projects that are created new or reopened. Essentially I would like to avoid any repetitive clicking whenever I switch context (project).

I'm really used to Eclipse storing the settings tied to the workspace and when a new project is created they have the same settings.
